sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CHIPOTLE ANNOUNCES FREE DOUBLE PROTEIN OFFER ON TUESDAY, JANUARY 13 TO POWER PROTEIN GOALS
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill is offering free double protein on bowls, burritos, and salads for Rewards members on Tuesday, January 13, 2026. This promotion, available digitally with code PROTEIN, aims to support customers' fitness goals as part of Chipotle's focus on high-protein menu items. The offer includes various protein options and is valid in the US and Canada.

feed.title: Moderna Provides Business and Pipeline Updates at 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference
feed.summary: Moderna announced significant financial and pipeline updates at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference, expecting $1.9 billion in 2025 revenue and reduced operating expenses. The company anticipates up to 10% revenue growth in 2026, driven by new product launches and pivotal trial data readouts in oncology, rare disease, and infectious disease, including potential first approvals for flu and flu/COVID combination products. Moderna is also advancing its oncology and rare disease therapeutic candidates with several clinical trials underway and expected data readouts in 2026.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb’s Camzyos shows positive results in adolescent oHCM trial By Investing.com
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb announced positive results from its Phase 3 SCOUT-HCM trial for Camzyos in adolescents with obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (oHCM). The drug met its primary endpoint, showing significant reduction in Valsalva left ventricular outflow tract gradient, with a safety profile consistent with adult use. The company plans to present detailed results and discuss the data with health authorities, highlighting potential new treatment options for this serious rare disease in adolescents.

feed.title: Abivax climbs on Lilly takeover speculation
feed.summary: Shares in Abivax rose significantly following a French media report suggesting a potential €15 billion takeover by Eli Lilly. The speculation stems from Abivax's positive phase 3 results for obefazimod in ulcerative colitis, a drug they believe could be a future market leader in inflammatory bowel disease. Abivax's CEO noted the company would need a partner for international distribution, while Lilly is reportedly seeking guidance on French foreign investment control.

feed.title: Henry Schein picks new CEO with significant healthcare distribution experience
feed.summary: Henry Schein has appointed Frederick M. Lowery as its new CEO, effective March 2, 2026. Lowery succeeds Stanley M. Bergman, who is stepping down after more than 35 years but will remain as chairman of the board to ensure a smooth transition. Lowery brings over two decades of healthcare industry experience, including previous leadership roles at Thermo Fisher Scientific, and is expected to guide Henry Schein through its next phase of growth.

feed.title: New cancer antibody deal brings $650M push against advanced solid tumors
feed.summary: AbbVie and RemeGen have signed an exclusive licensing agreement for RC148, a PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody designed for advanced solid tumors. AbbVie will pay RemeGen an upfront fee of $650 million, with potential milestone payments reaching up to $4.95 billion, plus tiered royalties on net sales outside Greater China. This deal enhances AbbVie's oncology portfolio and allows for exploration of RC148 in combination with its existing antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) programs.

feed.title: Dexcom Reports Preliminary, Unaudited Results for the Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 and Initial 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Dexcom, Inc. reported strong preliminary, unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025, with total revenue exceeding guidance and significant growth in both domestic and international markets. The company also provided a positive outlook for 2026, anticipating continued revenue growth and favorable non-GAAP gross profit and operating margins, driven by increased CGM awareness, the rollout of Stelo, and international expansion. Dexcom plans to release its audited full results on February 12, 2026.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Defines SHIELD Framework to Secure Vibecoding
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced the SHIELD framework, a set of best practices designed to secure applications developed using AI-driven vibecoding techniques. The framework addresses the increasing cybersecurity risks associated with the rapid adoption of vibecoding tools, which can be exploited for malicious code injection and data exfiltration. SHIELD emphasizes principles like separation of duties, human oversight for critical code, robust input/output validation, the use of security-focused helper models, least agency, and defensive technical controls to mitigate these risks.

feed.title: Global Payments completes Worldpay acquisition, divests Issuer Solutions By Investing.com
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. has completed its acquisition of Worldpay from FIS and GTCR, while simultaneously divesting its Issuer Solutions business to FIS. This strategic move transforms Global Payments into a pure-play commerce solutions provider, significantly expanding its capabilities and global reach. The company anticipates maintaining investment-grade credit ratings and aims to reduce adjusted net leverage within 18 to 24 months, while leveraging cross-selling opportunities between its and Worldpay's offerings.
